item.title: Home First Finance Declines 10.31%: Mixed Technicals and Fair Valuation Shape Week
item.description: Home First Finance Company India Ltd experienced a challenging week, with its stock price falling 10.31% from ₹1,200.00 to ₹1,076.25, significantly underperforming the broader Sensex, which declined 2.63% over the same period. The week was marked by a shift to a mildly bearish technical trend and a valuation recalibration from expensive to fair, reflecting evolving market sentiment amid sector volatility and broader market pressures. Time: 13 minutes ago

item.title: Stove Kraft Ltd Gains 0.58%: 3 Key Factors Driving the Week’s Mixed Momentum
item.description: Stove Kraft Ltd closed the week with a modest gain of 0.58%, ending at Rs.551.95 on 15 May 2026, despite a volatile trading week marked by sharp intraday swings and mixed financial signals. The stock outperformed the Sensex, which declined 2.63% over the same period, reflecting relative resilience amid sectoral and market headwinds. Key developments including a negative financial trend report, a quality downgrade, and a strong intraday surge shaped the stock’s trajectory this week. Time: 13 minutes ago

item.title: MTAR Technologies Surges 11.70%: 5 Key Drivers Behind the Rally
item.description: MTAR Technologies Ltd delivered a strong weekly performance, rising 11.70% from Rs.6,472.60 to Rs.7,230.05 between 11 and 15 May 2026, significantly outperforming the Sensex which declined 2.63% over the same period. The stock’s rally was marked by multiple new 52-week and all-time highs, robust quarterly results, and sustained institutional buying, reflecting strong momentum amid a cautious broader market. Time: 13 minutes ago

item.title: Easy Trip Planners Ltd’s Volatile Week: -0.63% Price Change Amid Strong Volume and Mixed Signals
item.description: Easy Trip Planners Ltd experienced a turbulent trading week from 11 to 15 May 2026, closing marginally lower by 0.63% at Rs.7.91 despite a strong midweek rally. The stock notably outperformed the broader Sensex, which declined 2.63% over the same period, reflecting selective investor interest amid a cautious market environment. Key events included a sharp 7.48% intraday surge on 13 May and an exceptional volume spike on 14 May, signalling renewed momentum despite a prevailing ‘Strong Sell’ rating from MarketsMOJO. Time: 13 minutes ago

item.title: Davangere Sugar Company Ltd Gains 4.89%: 2 Key Factors Driving the Week
item.description: Davangere Sugar Company Ltd closed the week with a notable 4.89% gain, outperforming the Sensex which declined by 2.63%. Despite a challenging start marked by a technical downgrade to a Strong Sell rating, the stock saw a remarkable surge in volume and price momentum midweek, signalling increased investor interest amid mixed technical signals. Time: 13 minutes ago

item.title: Craftsman Automation Ltd Falls 5.98%: Valuation Upgrade Amid Market Weakness
item.description: Craftsman Automation Ltd experienced a challenging week from 11 to 15 May 2026, with its stock price declining by 5.98% to close at Rs.8,462.90, underperforming the Sensex which fell 2.63% over the same period. Despite a strong upgrade to a 'Strong Buy' rating early in the week, the stock faced persistent selling pressure amid broader market weakness, reflecting a complex interplay between positive fundamental reassessments and volatile market conditions. Time: 13 minutes ago

item.title: Kalyan Jewellers Falls 16.81%: 5 Key Factors Behind the Sharp Weekly Decline
item.description: Kalyan Jewellers India Ltd endured a challenging week from 11 to 15 May 2026, with its stock price plunging 16.81% to close at Rs.353.25, significantly underperforming the Sensex’s 2.63% decline. The week was marked by sharp intraday falls, new 52-week lows, and heightened derivatives activity signalling bearish market sentiment amid broader sectoral and market pressures. Time: 13 minutes ago

item.title: Nazara Technologies Gains 11.87%: 5 Key Factors Driving the Surge
item.description: Nazara Technologies Ltd delivered a strong weekly performance, surging 11.87% from Rs.268.25 to Rs.300.10, significantly outperforming the Sensex which declined 2.63% over the same period. The week was marked by mixed financial results, valuation shifts, and exceptional trading activity culminating in a robust intraday rally on 15 May 2026, reflecting heightened market interest amid ongoing operational challenges. Time: 13 minutes ago

item.title: Suryoday Small Finance Bank Ltd Falls 7.07%: 6 Key Factors Driving the Weekly Decline
item.description: Suryoday Small Finance Bank Ltd experienced a challenging week, with its share price declining by 7.07% from Rs.180.25 to Rs.167.50 between 11 and 15 May 2026. Despite this, the stock outperformed the broader Sensex, which fell 2.63% over the same period. The week was marked by significant technical developments, quality upgrades, valuation shifts, and a new 52-week high, all contributing to a complex market narrative for this micro-cap banking stock. Time: 14 minutes ago

item.title: Deep Industries Ltd Falls 2.75%: Mixed Technicals and Q4 Loss Shape the Week
item.description: Deep Industries Ltd experienced a challenging week, closing down 2.75% to Rs.445.10 against a Sensex decline of 2.63%. The stock showed mixed technical signals early in the week before a sharp Q4 loss announcement on 15 May 2026 intensified selling pressure. Despite short-term resilience midweek, the overall trend reflected cautious investor sentiment amid sector headwinds and operational setbacks. Time: 14 minutes ago

item.title: Shyam Metalics & Energy Ltd Declines 2.91%: Mixed Signals Amid Record Quarter and Valuation Shifts
item.description: Shyam Metalics & Energy Ltd’s stock declined by 2.91% over the week ending 15 May 2026, closing at Rs.877.40 compared to Rs.903.70 the previous Friday. This underperformance slightly lagged the Sensex’s 2.63% drop, reflecting a week of mixed signals amid strong quarterly results, technical momentum shifts, and valuation recalibrations within a volatile iron and steel sector. Time: 14 minutes ago

item.title: Krishna Institute of Medical Sciences Ltd Gains 6.14%: Valuation Shifts and Market Dynamics in Focus
item.description: Krishna Institute of Medical Sciences Ltd (KIMS) delivered a notable weekly gain of 6.14%, closing at Rs.761.60 on 15 May 2026, despite a broadly bearish market environment where the Sensex declined by 2.63%. The stock demonstrated resilience with strong intraday highs and significant volatility, reflecting a complex interplay of valuation concerns and positive price momentum throughout the week. Time: 14 minutes ago

item.title: G R Infraprojects Declines 4.49%: Margin Pressures and Market Volatility Shape Week
item.description: G R Infraprojects Ltd experienced a challenging week on the bourses, closing down 4.49% from Rs.980.65 on 8 May to Rs.936.65 on 15 May 2026. This decline outpaced the Sensex’s 2.63% fall over the same period, reflecting investor concerns amid margin compression despite record quarterly sales and heightened volatility triggered by overnight news and a significant gap down on 12 May. Time: 14 minutes ago

item.title: Clean Science & Technology Ltd Falls 13.99%: Margin Pressures and Stabilised Trend Define Week
item.description: Clean Science & Technology Ltd endured a challenging week, with its share price declining 13.99% from Rs.892.70 to Rs.767.80, significantly underperforming the Sensex’s 2.63% fall over the same period. Despite a brief stabilisation in quarterly financial trends and a marginal intraday recovery midweek, persistent margin pressures and subdued profitability weighed heavily on investor sentiment throughout the week. Time: 14 minutes ago

item.title: Eternal Ltd Falls 5.97%: Key Market Pressures and Rebound Signals This Week
item.description: Eternal Ltd’s shares declined by 5.97% over the week ending 15 May 2026, underperforming the Sensex’s 2.63% fall. The stock faced sustained selling pressure early in the week, hitting an intraday low on 11 May before staging a partial recovery on 14 May with a 3.13% surge. Despite this rebound, the week closed with the stock down at Rs.241.05, reflecting ongoing volatility amid mixed technical signals and a cautious market environment. Time: 15 minutes ago

item.title: Alivus Life Sciences Declines 6.78%: Key Financial Trends and Market Reactions
item.description: Alivus Life Sciences Ltd experienced a challenging week on the BSE, with its stock price declining by 6.78% from Rs.1,120.90 on 8 May to Rs.1,044.85 on 15 May 2026. This underperformance contrasted with the broader Sensex, which fell 2.63% over the same period. Despite reporting record quarterly sales and strong profit after tax (PAT) growth, the company’s financial trend score flattened, reflecting investor caution amid margin compression concerns and a subdued near-term outlook. Time: 15 minutes ago

item.title: Gretex Corporate Services Ltd Gains 2.17%: 3 Key Factors Driving the Rally
item.description: Gretex Corporate Services Ltd closed the week with a 2.17% gain, rising from Rs.380.55 on 8 May to Rs.388.80 on 15 May 2026, outperforming the Sensex which declined by 2.63% over the same period. The stock demonstrated resilience amid volatile market conditions, buoyed by a quality grade upgrade, new 52-week highs, and sustained technical momentum. Time: 15 minutes ago

item.title: Meghmani Organics Declines 11.07% Amid Margin Pressures and Mixed Technical Signals
item.description: Meghmani Organics Ltd experienced a challenging week on the bourses, with its stock price declining by 11.07% from ₹58.47 to ₹52.00, significantly underperforming the Sensex which fell 2.63% over the same period. The week was marked by a series of bearish technical signals, disappointing quarterly results revealing margin pressures, and a mixed financial trend that weighed heavily on investor sentiment. Time: 15 minutes ago

item.title: Vijaya Diagnostic Centre Ltd Gains 4.45%: 4 Key Factors Driving the Week’s Momentum
item.description: Vijaya Diagnostic Centre Ltd closed the week ending 15 May 2026 with a 4.45% gain, rising from Rs.1,278.15 to Rs.1,335.05, outperforming the Sensex which declined 2.63% over the same period. The stock demonstrated resilience and strong momentum amid a broadly bearish market, driven by multiple technical milestones and sector-specific strength. Time: 15 minutes ago

item.title: Acutaas Chemicals Ltd’s Resilient Week: -0.09% Price Change Amid Market Volatility
item.description: Acutaas Chemicals Ltd experienced a marginal decline of 0.09% over the week ending 15 May 2026, closing at Rs.2,730.80. This performance contrasts sharply with the broader Sensex, which fell 2.63% during the same period. The stock demonstrated notable resilience, hitting multiple 52-week and all-time highs between 11 and 14 May, underscoring strong underlying fundamentals and sustained institutional confidence amid a challenging market backdrop. Time: 15 minutes ago
